symptomSummary: After ~30+ invocations, evolve stops acting on recent failures and regresses quality by targeting older issues.
rootCauseHypothesis: InvocationRepository.forRevision queries with ORDER BY date ASC LIMIT 25; once a revision has more than 25 invocations the query always returns the oldest 25, permanently excluding recent feedback from the evolve pipeline.
fixHypothesis: Change the forRevision query to ORDER BY date DESC LIMIT N so the telemetry window always reflects the most recent invocations; callers needing chronological order can reverse the slice in application code.

primaryLocation.rationale: ASC ordering + LIMIT means oldest rows win once the table exceeds the window size — exactly the threshold (~30) the user observes.

candidateLocations.rationale: Passes telemetryWindow (default 25) to invocationsForRevision with no recency filter; adding a since-timestamp option here would mitigate the bug even without fixing the repo layer.
Interface exposes only a limit param; adding a since option is the clean long-term fix.
Reference implementation may have a parallel copy of the same ascending-order bug used by the admin UI path.
Could compound the problem if it re-sorts or truncates the already-stale list before prompting, but unlikely to be the root cause.
